name: The Perfect Hard Boiled Egg
recipeInstructions:
Note: Water should cover the eggs by 1 inch, so use a tall pan and limit cooking to 2 dozen eggs at a time.
1. Place the eggs in the pan and add the specified amount of cold water. Bring to a boil over high heat, then remove from heat, cover the pan, and let sit for exactly 17 minutes.
2. Transfer the eggs to a bowl filled with ice cubes and water. Chill for 2 minutes while bringing the cooking water back to a boil. (The 2-minute chilling shrinks the egg body from the shell.)
3. Return the eggs (in batches of 6) to the boiling water, bring to a boil again, and let boil for 10 seconds. Remove the eggs and place them back into the ice water. Chilling promptly after each step prevents a dark line from forming. If time allows, keep the eggs in the ice water for an additional 15 to 20 minutes before peeling. The peeled eggs will stay fresh in the refrigerator, submerged in water in an uncovered container, for 2 to 3 days.
